analysis_result: At 120 hours, HSC1λ-gPTCH1 knockout cells had mean cell count = 316,666.67 (SD 20,816.66, N=3), compared to HSC1λ-gGFP control mean = 223,333.33 (SD 10,488.09, N=3). PTCH1 knockout cells had ~41.8% more cells (ratio PTCH1/GFP = 1.418; difference = 93,333). Welch's t-test: t=6.94, df=2.95, p=0.0065 (significantly greater).
denominator_used: N = 3 replicates per group at 120 h (HSC1λ-gGFP control: N=3; HSC1λ-gPTCH1 knockout: N=3), as tabulated in MOESM10 Fig 5b source data.
assumptions_made: Used mean and SD values pre-computed in the source data for the 120-hour row; Welch's t-test was computed from summary statistics (mean, SD, N) rather than raw replicate values since only summary stats are present in the sheet; assumed two-sided test with unequal variances.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 5b' (8 rows x 7 cols).
  - Layout: columns B-D = HSC1λ-gGFP (Mean, SD, N); columns E-G = HSC1λ-gPTCH1 (Mean, SD, N); column A = Time (Hrs) with rows 0, 24, 48, 72, 96, 120.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Row at Time = 120 hours (last row of the time course).

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - HSC1λ-gGFP at 120 h: Mean=223333.3333, SD=10488.08848, N=3.
  - HSC1λ-gPTCH1 at 120 h: Mean=316666.6667, SD=20816.65999, N=3.

D. COMPUTATION
  - Read mean/SD/N at 120 h directly from the sheet.
  - Ratio = PTCH1_mean / GFP_mean.
  - Difference = PTCH1_mean - GFP_mean.
  - Percent increase = (PTCH1 - GFP) / GFP * 100.
  - Welch's t-test from summary statistics:
      SE = sqrt(SD1^2/N1 + SD2^2/N2)
      t = (M2 - M1) / SE
      df from Welch-Satterthwaite.

E. RESULT
  - HSC1λ-gPTCH1: mean = 316,666.67 cells (SD = 20,816.66, N=3).
  - HSC1λ-gGFP:   mean = 223,333.33 cells (SD = 10,488.09, N=3).
  - Ratio (PTCH1/GFP) = 1.418; PTCH1 KO has ~41.8% more cells than control.
  - Absolute difference = 93,333 cells.
  - Welch's t-test (from summary stats): t = 6.935, df = 2.95, p = 0.0065.

F. CAVEATS
  - Raw replicate counts are not provided in MOESM10; only per-timepoint mean/SD/N were tabulated, so the t-test is computed from summary statistics.
  - Small N (3 per group) gives limited power but result is clearly significant at p<0.05.
  - Units: absolute cell counts as recorded by the authors (exact measurement protocol not specified in the source data sheet itself).
